what_it_does: Implements an action-class governance policy (AUTO/SURFACE/CONFIRM) and an Inbox that persists surfaced operator decisions, with locking to close an id-allocation race and a threaded self-check.
observations: POLICY maps action classes to AUTO (cheap/reversible/internal), SURFACE (meaningful/recoverable), or CONFIRM (irreversible/expensive/external); unknown classes default to CONFIRM.
LOCKED forever-confirm set includes source_data, external, and frozen_contract.
guard() runs a callable immediately for AUTO, runs and records a default-proceed surfaced note for SURFACE, and requires confirmed=True for CONFIRM or else surfaces a gate and raises GovernanceError.
Inbox.surface() allocates unique ids by scanning persisted records and wraps allocate→mint→save in store.surfaced_lock() to close the T1-RACE create-path.
Inbox supports a separate lifecycle status field (inbox/presented/responded/resolved/requeue/implemented) without overloading resolved.
Surfaced items created under COMPANY_TEST_RUN are tagged test_origin at creation time.
surface_review() stores review decisions in the same surfaced store with status='inbox' and origin responsive|generative.
resolve() is documented as operator-only and writes the resolved field under the store lock.
A threaded self-check under __main__ verifies that concurrent surface() calls produce no lost updates and no duplicate ids.
standouts: Extensive inline comments tie every policy choice to named decisions (S7/D4/D7, T1-RACE, T3-HYGIENE, A, W4, GC10, WS1), making the file self-documenting.
The race-fix is explicitly scoped to in-process concurrency and openly notes the remaining cross-process gap.
decision_build is AUTO only for dispatch on operator approval; implemented results are still surfaced for review, so AUTO does not mean review-free.
conventions: Module-level constants and a policy table dict.
Fail-loud exceptions for missing confirmation, unknown status, and missing items.
Read-modify-write surfaced operations wrapped in store.surfaced_lock().
Test-origin tagging driven by an environment variable at creation time.
Self-test runs only when the file is executed directly.
concerns: guard() accepts a caller-supplied confirmed boolean; the file relies on convention, not a mechanical channel check, to prevent an agent from setting it.
resolve() is labeled operator-only but contains no runtime enforcement of the calling channel in this file.
Cross-process surfaced-item creation remains unprotected, as acknowledged in the comments.
_next_index scans and regex-parses every persisted surfaced id on each create, which may slow down as the inbox grows.
notes: This file is the policy engine and decision inbox: it classifies actions by reversibility, cost, and externality, then persists and resolves operator decisions with race-safe id allocation.
questions: What is the exact contract of store.surfaced_lock(), save_surfaced(), get_surfaced(), and list_surfaced()?
How does the system ensure resolve() is reachable only from the operator/UI channel and not from the agent/MCP face?
What compensation or rollback path exists if a SURFACE action proceeds by default and the operator later rejects it?
How are action_class strings assigned to concrete operations elsewhere in the codebase?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as 'act-unwatched policy + surfaced-decision gate'; the file matches that purpose and adds detailed rationale, concurrency fixes, and a self-check.
apparent_themes: Operator authorization as the single source of truth for high-stakes actions.
Auditability and non-silent failure for surfaced decisions.
Explicit scoping of concurrency guarantees and known gaps.
intention_signals: The file tries to make policy enforcement transparent, self-documenting, and mechanically safe under concurrent surfacing, while keeping operator approval as the unavoidable gate for irreversible actions.
novelty: The combination of a posture table with per-action-class rationale, a unified inbox/decision store, and an explicit in-process lock fix backed by a threaded self-check is relatively distinctive.
contribution: Provides a centralized, readable policy table and a race-safe surfaced-decision inbox that a fused system would keep as the place where action classes are classified and operator decisions are persisted.
summary_for_embedding: Governance policy and surfaced-decision inbox implementing AUTO/SURFACE/CONFIRM posture classes, race-safe id allocation for persisted decisions, and operator-only resolution.
intention_for_embedding: To gate action execution by reversibility, cost, and externality and route every consequential decision through a persisted, operator-resolvable inbox.

symbols.does: Exception raised when a CONFIRM action is attempted without confirmation.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass used by guard() to fail loudly when an action requiring operator confirmation is invoked without confirmed=True.

symbols.does: Returns the governance posture for an action class.
symbols.description: Looks up action_class in POLICY and returns AUTO, SURFACE, or CONFIRM; unknown classes default to CONFIRM.

symbols.does: Executes a callable according to the action's governance posture.
symbols.description: Runs do() immediately for AUTO, runs and surfaces a default-proceed note for SURFACE, and either runs do() when confirmed or surfaces a gate and raises GovernanceError for CONFIRM.

symbols.does: Persists and manages surfaced operator decisions.
symbols.description: Wraps a store to create, list, get, update status, and resolve surfaced decisions with unique ids and lifecycle tracking.

symbols.does: Initializes the inbox with a store and an in-memory counter.
symbols.description: Stores the shared store and sets _n to 0; _next_index later reconciles the counter against persisted ids.

symbols.does: Computes the next surfaced item index from persisted records.
symbols.description: Scans store.list_surfaced() for ids matching s<N>- and returns max+1 so process restart cannot reuse an index.

symbols.does: Creates and persists a new surfaced decision item.
symbols.description: Allocates a unique id under store.surfaced_lock(), builds a record with action, payload, default, resolved, optional status/origin/test_origin, and saves it to the store.

symbols.does: Surfaces a review decision with lifecycle status.
symbols.description: Calls surface() for action 'review' with default reject, resolved None, status 'inbox', and origin responsive|generative so it remains a live escalation until resolved.

symbols.does: Advances a surfaced item's lifecycle status.
symbols.description: Validates status against REVIEW_STATUSES, loads the item under surfaced_lock, updates the status field without touching resolved, and saves it back.

symbols.does: Returns a single surfaced item by id or None.
Operator-only resolution of a surfaced decision.
symbols.description: Loads the item under surfaced_lock, writes the resolved choice and optional reason, and saves it back.

symbols.does: Checks whether a surfaced item was approved.
symbols.description: Returns True if the item exists and its resolved field equals 'approve'.

symbols.does: Self-check helper that runs concurrent surface() calls.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary FsStore, releases n_threads together to surface items, and returns the count and parsed indices to detect lost updates or duplicate ids.

symbols.does: Single thread worker for the race self-check.
symbols.description: Waits on a barrier, calls surface() with a class based on same_class, and records any exception.
Increments a global pass counter and prints ok if cond is true, otherwise raises with the given label.
